title: [v5.8] test/system: fix quadlet - rootfs on kernel 7.1
body:
As found in fedora openQA testing on the kernel update this test fails due a selinux denial.

The kernel now correctly checks the backing fs selinux context in all cases. See kernel commit 82544d36b172 (selinux: fix overlayfs mmap() and mprotect() access checks).

The test tries to access another container so the selinux level will be different from the quadlet roofs container and therefore fail to access the files.

To fix this we should be able to just use the regular image mount which has a level all containers can access. The comment that this is not parallel safe is untrue, mounts are reference counted so this should not get unmounted all of the sudden by another test.

In fact we have another  --rootfs test case where we do this and it passed in openQA, see "podman run - check workdir".

Fixes: #28602


(cherry picked from commit 12d40777f020f0adc40461d28ed6e7885a7dcf98)
